title: APUSH DBQ Graphic Organizer Template
description: Use this template for all DBQ practice essays. Rows for each document: claim, evidence, HAPP analysis, connection to thesis.

title: AP Exam Scoring Rubric - DBQ, LEQ, SAQ
description: Official College Board scoring rubrics for all three free-response question types. Study these carefully - they explain exactly how points are earned.

title: Labor History Primary Source Packet
description: Primary sources covering Triangle Shirtwaist Fire 1911, Lawrence Mill Strike 1912, Pullman Strike 1894, and 1919 Boston Police Strike. Includes scaffolding questions.

title: US Foreign Policy 1890-1920 Document Set
description: 7 primary sources for the DBQ: McKinley War Message, Anti-Imperialist League platform, Roosevelt Corollary, Zimmermann Telegram, Wilson War Address, AFL statement, and newspaper editorial.

title: Revolutionary Era Pamphlets Collection
description: Digital collection: Common Sense (Thomas Paine, 1776), Letters of a Pennsylvania Farmer (Dickinson, 1767), Rights of the British Colonies (Otis, 1764), and Loyalist response documents.

title: APUSH Period Review Guides - Periods 1-9
description: One-page review guide for each APUSH period. Key events, themes, and sample short-answer prompts. Prepared by Mr. Russo based on College Board curriculum framework.

title: Civil Rights Movement Primary Source Packet
description: Primary sources: MLK Letter from Birmingham Jail, SNCC Position Paper 1966, Voting Rights Act excerpts, Malcolm X speech, and Freedom Riders testimony.

title: Cold War Unit Vocabulary and Key Concepts
description: Vocabulary list for Unit 8: containment, domino theory, NSC-68, détente, Mutually Assured Destruction, military-industrial complex, proxy war.

title: Sample AP Exam DBQ - Scored at 7/7
description: Annotated example of a full-credit DBQ response. Teacher comments explain why each rubric point was earned. Use this as a model for your own essays.

title: Gilded Age and Labor Movement Reading Guide
description: Guided reading questions for Chapters 18-20. Covers Carnegie steel, Homestead Strike, AFL formation, Pullman Strike, and the rise of progressive labor legislation.

title: Jacksonian Era Primary Sources
description: Sources for the LEQ: Jackson Veto Message 1832, Nullification Crisis documents, Indian Removal Act debates, and Whig party response pamphlets.

title: 1919 Boston Police Strike Primary Source Set
description: Boston-area sources: Mayor Peters proclamation, AFL statement, Boston Globe editorial Sept 10, BPD patrolman firsthand account, Governor Coolidge statement.

title: APUSH 5-Year Exam Score Data by Topic
description: Aggregated College Board data showing historically low-scoring question types. Used to prioritize our final review sessions for April and May.

title: Imperialism Primary Sources - Both Sides
description: Pro-imperialism: Beveridge Senate Speech 1900, Mahan Sea Power excerpts. Anti-imperialism: Mark Twain commentary, Schurz speech, Aguinaldo proclamation.

title: LEQ Complexity Point Examples
description: Three annotated examples of essays that earned the complexity point. Shows how to make meaningful connections across time periods, themes, or geographic areas.

title: Vietnam War Era Primary Sources
description: Sources: Gulf of Tonkin Resolution, LBJ Johns Hopkins Speech, SDS Port Huron Statement, Vietnam Veterans Against the War testimony, Nixon Vietnamization speech.

title: Colonial Era Map Collection
description: Maps for the colonial period unit: land grants, trade routes, colonial regions (New England, Middle, Southern), and British territorial claims after 1763.

title: Gettysburg Battle Analysis - Pre-Trip Materials
description: Reading materials for the May History Club Gettysburg trip. Includes battle maps, casualty statistics, key unit positions, and primary accounts from Union and Confederate soldiers.

title: Progressive Era Reform Document Collection
description: Sources: Upton Sinclair excerpt from The Jungle, NAACP founding documents, Ida Tarbell muckraking article, Jane Addams Hull House description, Muller v. Oregon decision excerpts.

title: WWII Homefront Primary Sources
description: Sources: FDR Four Freedoms Speech, Executive Order 9066 (Japanese internment), WWII Rationing Poster collection, Rosie the Riveter propaganda analysis, Double V Campaign materials.
